_comment: Frozen TerminalBench subset for routine external-benchmark runs. Inclusion criteria: (1) real terminal-bench-2 task IDs at the pinned dataset_commit; (2) environment.cpus == 1 and memory <= 2G so trials fit single-host runners; (3) verifier.timeout_sec and agent.timeout_sec both <= 1200; (4) metadata.expert_time_estimate_min <= 180; (5) no 'no-verified-solution' tag; (6) stratified across software-engineering / security / file-operations / system-administration / data-processing / debugging so a small per-release sweep still covers the major task families. Bumping version requires a fresh baseline.
selection_rule: Same selection as scripts/benchmark/task-subset-v2.yaml — filtered TB2 tasks with fixed category quotas (5/3/2/2/2/1) sorted by difficulty then ID. Use this JSON form for cmd/bench --external=termbench; the YAML form is preserved for the evidence-grade comparison script.

tasks.rationale: Async/debug-heavy code fix; exercises read, edit, and test loops.
Build-oriented task that stresses shell discipline and targeted fixes.
Mixed extraction and coding workflow; broadens beyond plain text edits.
Git-oriented remediation task aligned with repository tooling workflows.
Terminal-centric task that can expose shell exploration anti-patterns.
High-signal secure-editing task for structured edit and validation behavior.
Troubleshooting-heavy security task with file and shell interaction.
Smaller security task that helps detect regressions on easier remediation work.
Recovery task with binary/file handling and verification pressure.
File-inspection task that stresses targeted reads over shell thrash.
Short but high-signal config task that exercises precise shell usage.
Multi-branch git workflow useful for navigation and command discipline.
Text-processing/report task aligned with read-edit-run loops.
Multi-input transformation task that stresses structured reasoning over bash search.
Build/debug task that checks diagnosis quality and fix verification.
Canonical TB hello-world; cheap signal that the adapter is wired end-to-end.
Easy programming smoke task; useful as a low-variance harness sanity check.
Easy algorithmic task; complements the hard-mode coverage above.
Trivial task to keep the easy tail represented.
Trivial task; helps confirm even cheap models clear something.
